answer:
A bitloop is defined as a bit string (or bitstring) whose ends are connected to form a loop. This means that the sequence of bits is continuous and circular, with no beginning or end. The set of all bitstrings with \( n \) bits can be partitioned by bitloops, and these bitloops can further be organized into chains and graphs. Each bitloop has a unique pattern, which is another bitloop that iterates within it a whole number of times, matching every bit in the bitloop with none left over. Bitloops are typically represented by bitstrings enclosed in parentheses to indicate that the first and last bits are adjacent. <sup>1</sup>  
  
<sup>1</sup> [Bitloops](https://symbolfigures.io/bitloops.html)
